name: Baidu Pan
summary: Baidu's large-scale cloud storage service with multi-device backup, file sharing, iPhone/Mac apps, an official PCS open platform, and very large paid expansion tiers.

free_model.notes: Official pages indicate ordinary accounts and free quota mechanics exist, but the accessible evidence does not publish one stable free-storage number, and Bangladesh registration/SMS access failed in user testing.

max_file_size.notes: Baidu's official help center says ordinary users can upload up to 1 GB per file on the web and up to 4 GB per file in the Windows and Mac desktop clients. Paid members can upload up to 10 GB per file in the desktop clients, and super members up to 20 GB.

max_file_size_account.notes: Baidu's official help center says ordinary account holders can upload up to 1 GB per file on the web and up to 4 GB per file in the Windows and Mac desktop clients. Paid members can upload up to 10 GB per file in the desktop clients, and super members up to 20 GB.

retention.notes: Files remain until deleted. Baidu's official help center says deleted files stay in the recycle bin for 10 days on ordinary accounts, 15 days for members, and 30 days for super members before permanent deletion.

storage.notes: The cited first-party pages still do not cleanly expose one stable default free-storage quota in accessible text. Current official help says every account has a free quota, mobile-app login can grant surprise bonus capacity, and some promotional capacity can expire, but it still does not publish one reliable base free number we can promote into the dataset.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth is conditional. Baidu's current official help says the Mac client does not impose upload or download speed throttling, but the cited first-party pages still do not publish one clean universal free-user traffic quota across web, desktop, and sharing flows.

account.benefits: A Baidu account is required for cloud backup, syncing, link sharing, and API-authorized access across devices, but current user-provided tests indicate the registration and SMS-login flows are not working reliably for Bangladeshi phone numbers.

developer.notes: Baidu publishes an official PCS open platform for personal cloud storage with file APIs, structured-data APIs, OAuth-based access, and SDK downloads, but the cited official pages still do not present a simple end-user CLI workflow.

security.notes: The App Store listing says the service uses multiple data centers and cites ISO27001 and ISO27018 security certifications. The official help center also says users can protect shared links with passwords and set automatic link expiry, but it still does not claim end-to-end encryption.

name: Cloudflare R2

retention.notes: R2 does not impose an automatic expiry for Standard objects by default; objects remain until deleted or lifecycle rules are configured.

storage.notes: The free tier includes 10 GB-month per month for Standard storage.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth is conditional. Cloudflare advertises zero egress fees for R2 object delivery, while the official pricing and limits pages publish storage, operation, and platform limits rather than one simple per-user bandwidth quota.

account.benefits: A Cloudflare account is required to create buckets, API tokens, custom domains, and public bucket settings.

name: DirectUpload
summary: Long-running German image host with free anonymous uploads, short custom auto-delete options, and a 1-year minimum storage period.

free_model.notes: This candidate has previously been verified for a free, guest, or free-account path. Recheck first-party sources before publishing or editing the row.

max_file_size.notes: The current upload page lists a maximum of 8 MB per file.

max_file_size_account.notes: Registered members gain long-term image storage and management, but the cited public upload surface does not publish a separate higher per-file cap beyond the same 8 MB limit.

retention.notes: The homepage currently advertises a 1-year minimum storage duration, while uploaders can also choose shorter auto-delete periods during upload.

storage.notes: Storage is conditional. DirectUpload offers anonymous image hosting with selectable auto-delete periods, while registered members can store their images free and lifelong; the service does not publish a clean byte quota.

bandwidth.notes: Unlimited in normal use. DirectUpload's official FAQ says each image can be loaded as often as needed, regardless of how much traffic it generates, while the AGB reserve the right to auto-compress unusually high-traffic images to preserve service performance.

account.benefits: Anonymous uploads are supported; registered members can store images free and long-term.

developer.notes: The cited public pages do not document a public API or CLI workflow.

security.notes: The cited public pages focus on image hosting features and do not claim end-to-end encryption.

sources.label: Homepage
sources.notes: Current file-size limit, supported image formats, auto-delete options, and minimum storage duration.

sources.label: FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ stating that each image may be loaded as often as desired regardless of generated traffic, plus the current one-year anonymous retention guarantee and 8 MB file size.

sources.label: Rules & AGB
sources.notes: Official rules reserving the right to auto-compress a small fraction of unusually high-traffic images to preserve service performance.

allowed_file_types.notes: The current upload page supports JPG, GIF, PNG, WEBP, BMP, HEIC, and PDF uploads, with some formats converted automatically.

public_sharing.notes: Public link or share-page support is part of this verified candidate record; see the cited source notes for the current sharing surface.

name: Disroot Upload
summary: Privacy-focused temporary file sharing from Disroot, powered by Lufi and encrypted in the browser before upload.

free_model.notes: This candidate has previously been verified for a free, guest, or free-account path. Recheck first-party sources before publishing or editing the row.

max_file_size.notes: Maximum upload behavior is conditional. The current hosted upload page does not publish one hard maximum file size, but it does expose size-based retention rules for the hosted instance, including 0-96 MB, 96-954 MB, and 954 MB or larger files.

retention.notes: Retention is conditional. The current upload page lets users choose 24 hours, 7 days, 30 days, or 90 days, but the actual expiry becomes the smaller of the chosen delay and the instance's size-based server limits: 0-96 MB files are kept 90 days, 96-954 MB files are kept 15 days, and 954 MB or larger files are kept 2 days.

storage.notes: Disroot Upload is a temporary sharing service rather than persistent account storage. The official service page frames it as temporary encrypted file sharing with download and deletion links, not as a reusable cloud drive quota.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth is conditional. The official Disroot and hosted Lufi pages do not publish a numeric throughput cap, and effective transfer availability depends on the sender's chosen delay, the optional delete-at-first-download mode, and the instance's size-based retention limits.

account.benefits: No account is needed to upload files; users receive download and deletion links.

developer.notes: The cited current public pages do not document a public API or CLI workflow for this hosted instance.

security.notes: Disroot states files are encrypted in the browser before upload, so administrators cannot see the file contents.

sources.label: Upload Service
sources.notes: Service description, no-account upload flow, and privacy model.

sources.label: Lufi About
sources.notes: Hosted Lufi instance details and encrypted upload behavior.

sources.label: Hosted upload page
sources.notes: Current upload form showing available expiry choices and the hosted instance's size-based retention caps.

allowed_file_types.notes: The service describes general file hosting with browser-side encryption and references file name, size, and mimetype rather than a narrow allowlist.

public_sharing.notes: Public link or share-page support is part of this verified candidate record; see the cited source notes for the current sharing surface.

name: DooDrive
summary: Free account-based file host with 2 GB uploads, unlimited-storage marketing, inactivity-based deletion, download-manager support, and optional patron upgrades.

free_model.notes: The official FAQ says DooDrive is 100% free for all users, and the plan page lists the Standard plan as free forever.

max_file_size.notes: The official FAQ says each uploaded file must be less than 2 GB; the plan page has a conflicting 5 GB table, so the FAQ cap is used for the headline.

max_file_size_account.notes: The official FAQ says uploaded files greater than 2 GB are rejected; the plan page has a conflicting 5 GB table.

retention.notes: The plan page lists a 1-day file expiry and clarifies that only inactive files after a specified period are deleted; the FAQ also says files may be removed after inactivity.

storage.notes: The homepage and plan page advertise unlimited account storage, but no numeric free storage quota is published.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth is conditional. The homepage says downloads are not speed-limited and the plan page lists unlimited downloads, but no numeric transfer quota is published.

account.benefits: The public flow routes users to sign-up. Accounts provide file management, unlimited-storage marketing, download-manager support, and optional patron tools.

developer.notes: The cited first-party pages do not publish a public API or CLI upload workflow.

allowed_file_types.notes: The official FAQ says any file type may be uploaded if it follows the Terms of Service and size limit.

public_sharing.notes: The homepage describes pre-generated HTML code, website links, and social sharing.

security.notes: The cited pages describe secure cloud file storage but do not claim end-to-end encryption.

sources.label: DooDrive homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage verifies free cloud storage/sharing, account file manager, unlimited account-storage wording, no download speed limit, download-manager support, and public sharing tools.

sources.label: DooDrive FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ verifies 100% free service, inactivity-based deletion, unlimited number of files subject to terms and max size, any-file-type support, and a less-than-2-GB upload limit.

sources.label: DooDrive patron page
sources.notes: Official plan page lists the Standard plan as free forever with unlimited storage/downloads, file-expiry notes, ads, and optional patron upgrades.

bandwidth.notes: Old official WJunction posts advertised unlimited daily download traffic, but no current bandwidth policy can be verified.

account.benefits: The old service centered on registered uploader accounts, premium upgrades, affiliate payouts, and remote upload; no current account behavior is verified.

name: Dosya.co
summary: Turkish free file host with guest uploads up to 2 GB, registered free uploads up to 3 GB, 30 GB account storage, and inactivity-based deletion.

free_model.notes: The homepage and FAQ say files can be uploaded/shared for free and that free membership is available.

max_file_size.notes: The homepage and FAQ say visitors can upload files up to 2048 MB.

storage_account.notes: The homepage table says members get 30 GB of storage space.

bandwidth.notes: The FAQ says free members have download speed limits, but it does not publish a numeric transfer quota.

public_sharing.notes: The homepage describes uploading and sharing files, and the terms say uploaded files are not private and may be publicly accessible.

security.notes: The public pages are HTTPS, but the terms explicitly say uploaded files are not private and may be indexed or accessed publicly.

free_model.notes: Rejected during source review. Rejected because Egnyte is a paid business content platform with trials, not an ongoing free public file-hosting plan.

max_file_size.notes: Egnyte lists 100 GB file-size limits for Business/Team plans and 150 GB for Enterprise-tier plans; trial domains are separately limited.

retention.notes: Egnyte keeps three versions by default for a file, with admin/plan controls that may change this for eligible file types.

storage.notes: Trial domains are limited to 20 GB total storage; paid plans are business subscriptions rather than ongoing free public hosting.

bandwidth.notes: No public free-host bandwidth allowance applies; Egnyte documents paid-plan download/share restrictions rather than a free hosting transfer pool.

account.benefits: An Egnyte trial or paid business account is required for upload, sharing, governance, and collaboration features.

developer.notes: Egnyte has a developer platform, but this row remains rejected because the product is a paid business content platform rather than free-first public hosting.

allowed_file_types.notes: Egnyte supports general cloud content uploads but does not support active database files such as Exchange, QuickBooks, SQL, Oracle, or MS Access; trial domains also restrict risky file types such as ZIP, EXE, and BAT.

public_sharing.notes: Egnyte paid/trial accounts include controlled sharing features, but trial and newly created domains have extra restrictions on share-link email and URL creation.

security.notes: Security properties were not fully modeled because this lead was rejected for product-fit/free-model reasons.

sources.notes: Official help article says active database files such as Exchange, QuickBooks, SQL, Oracle, and MS Access are not supported.

sources.notes: Official help article says Egnyte has Public APIs and links to the developer portal, while noting API availability restrictions for trials and some legacy plans.

name: End2End.tech
summary: Open-source anonymous file uploader with optional end-to-end encryption, no standard login requirement, public API docs, and cURL upload/download/delete examples.

free_model.notes: The site presents a public anonymous uploader and API documentation that says registration is not required.

max_file_size.notes: The current about page says uploads are currently limited to 100 MB per file; the main UI still contains an older 2048 MB text string, so the more conservative current about-page value is used.

retention.notes: Retention is user-configurable. The uploader and API support setting a download deadline, but the cited pages do not publish a fixed default expiry.

storage.notes: End2End.tech is an anonymous file uploader, not a persistent personal storage account.

bandwidth.notes: The cited public pages do not publish a numeric bandwidth or transfer quota.

account.benefits: The API documentation says registration is not required for API use, while asking users to avoid excessive load and scraping.

developer.notes: Official API docs provide cURL examples for upload, download, and deletion plus options for download limits, deadlines, and VPN/Tor blocking.

allowed_file_types.notes: The uploader describes generic file uploads and does not publish a narrow extension allowlist or blocklist.

public_sharing.notes: The UI and API return download URLs, share URLs, and removal passwords for uploaded files.

security.notes: The homepage/about pages describe optional end-to-end encryption and no-log behavior; the about page says IP addresses are not recorded, though request history without IP addresses is retained for 30 days for attack prevention.

sources.notes: Official homepage exposes anonymous upload UI, optional E2E encryption password, download-count and deadline controls, VPN/Tor download blocking, share/delete outputs, GitHub link, and API docs link.

sources.notes: Seed table listed end2end as 2 GB / infinite retention; current official pages have conflicting UI/about limits, so the dataset uses the current about-page 100 MB value and treats retention as user-configurable.

name: Fileditch
summary: Anonymous file-sharing host with a no-auth HTTP API, 25 GB files, and low-disk cleanup only after 30 days of inactivity.

free_model.notes: This candidate has previously been verified for a free, guest, or free-account path. Recheck first-party sources before publishing or editing the row.

max_file_size.notes: The FAQ and API docs both state a 25 GB maximum file size.

retention.notes: Retention is conditional. FileDitch keeps files indefinitely while storage allows, and only removes the oldest files that have not been accessed in 30 days when disk space is genuinely needed.

storage.notes: Storage is conditional. The FAQ says there is no limit on the number of files you can upload, but files are only kept while capacity allows and inactive files can be reclaimed when disk space is genuinely needed.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth is conditional. FileDitch does not publish a transfer-volume allowance, but the FAQ says uploads are protected by rate limiting and automated abuse prevention, and hotlinking or embedding from external sites is not supported.

account.benefits: No account is required. FileDitch is fully anonymous and does not offer login-based upload tracking.

developer.notes: FileDitch documents simple no-auth HTTP uploads with curl, raw POST uploads, multipart uploads, and JSON responses.

security.notes: The public pages use HTTPS and say uploaded links are unlisted, but they explicitly say privacy is not guaranteed and do not claim end-to-end encryption.

name: Filestore.to
summary: German cloud-storage/file-sharing service with guest uploads up to 2 GB, generated public links, premium unlimited downloads, and legal restrictions on stored content.

free_model.notes: The homepage describes online storage as simple, fast, and free, and the upload page is publicly accessible.

max_file_size.notes: The official upload page says files can be chosen with a max of 2 GB.

retention.notes: The terms say free-user files may be deleted if volume is exceeded or the contract term ends, and that users have no claim to permanent storage; no fixed current free retention window is published.

storage.notes: The cited public pages describe cloud storage but do not publish a numeric free storage quota.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth is conditional. The premium page says premium is required for unrestricted ad-free downloads, but no numeric free transfer quota is published.

account.benefits: The upload page is public; registration and premium accounts add account/premium download workflows.

developer.notes: The cited public pages do not document a public API or command-line upload workflow.

allowed_file_types.notes: The terms frame uploads as user files such as movies, photos, and documents, while prohibiting malware, infringing, pornographic, violent, hate, personal-data, and other unlawful content.

public_sharing.notes: The terms say uploaded files are made available under a generated link.

security.notes: The public pages are HTTPS, but the terms state Filestore has no influence over who downloads user-uploaded files and do not claim end-to-end encryption.

sources.label: Filestore.to homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describes Filestore as cloud storage that is simple, fast, and free.

sources.label: Filestore.to upload page
sources.notes: Official upload page exposes public uploads and says users can choose files up to 2 GB.

sources.label: Filestore.to premium page
sources.notes: Official premium page says unrestricted, ad-free downloading requires a premium upgrade.

sources.label: Filestore.to terms
sources.notes: Official terms describe generated links, prohibited content, file deletion conditions, and lack of permanent-storage entitlement.

sources.label: PolyUploader supported-hosts table
sources.notes: Seed table listed Filestore as 2 GB / 15 days; current official upload page confirms 2 GB but the accessible terms do not publish a simple 15-day rule.

name: Fireload

name: Free Transfert
summary: Free's browser-based file transfer service with direct-link sharing, optional password protection, and a larger free allowance for Freebox subscribers.

free_model.notes: This candidate has previously been verified for a free, guest, or free-account path. Recheck first-party sources before publishing or editing the row.

max_file_size.notes: Official support currently states transfers are free up to 10 GB for non-Freebox users, while Freebox subscribers get unlimited volume.

max_file_size_account.notes: Maximum transfer size is conditional for account-linked / subscriber use. Official support says non-Freebox users are capped at 10 GB, while Freebox subscribers can transfer files without a published volume limit.

retention.notes: Official support says transfer validity can be set up to 30 days for Freebox subscribers and up to 7 days for non-Freebox users.

storage.notes: Free Transfert is explicitly described as provisional storage for sending large files to recipients, not a persistent cloud drive.

bandwidth.notes: Transfer allowance depends on subscriber status: Freebox subscribers can send files without a published volume cap, while non-subscribers are capped at 10 GB per transfer. The official pages do not publish a separate throughput limit.

account.benefits: The service is free to use from a browser, with expanded limits for Freebox subscribers.

developer.notes: The cited official support pages describe browser-based sharing only and do not document a public API or CLI flow.

security.notes: Free states transferred files are encrypted with HTTPS and stored in datacenters in Paris until expiration; the cited pages do not claim end-to-end encryption.

name: Fshare
summary: Vietnamese cloud storage and file-sharing service with 50 GB free storage, browser/email sharing, and official desktop and mobile apps.

free_model.notes: This candidate has previously been verified for a free, guest, or free-account path. Recheck first-party sources before publishing or editing the row.

max_file_size.notes: The cited public pages describe a 20 GB guest quick-send flow and 50 GB of free storage for registered users, but they do not publish a separate lower signed-in per-file cap. For a free account, the practical upload ceiling therefore follows the 50 GB storage quota.

retention.notes: Current Fshare policy updates say free-member files stored in the time-limited area get an extra 15 days of guaranteed retention from the earlier 30-day policy, bringing the inactive retention window to 45 days for files without downloads in that area.

storage.notes: Fshare currently gives registered free users 50 GB of storage.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth varies by user type. The features page publishes different free download speeds and waiting times, such as 150 KB/s for guests and 300 KB/s for free accounts, instead of one clean quota.

max_file_size_account.notes: For registered free Fshare accounts, the practical upload ceiling is the 50 GB storage quota.

account.benefits: A registered account unlocks the 50 GB free tier, password-protected files, favorite links, and Fshare mobile and desktop tooling.

developer.notes: The cited public consumer pages do not document a public API or CLI upload workflow.

security.notes: The cited public pages position Fshare as cloud storage and sharing, but they do not claim end-to-end encryption for the free service.

name: Hostuje

retention.notes: Object Storage does not impose automatic expiry by default; objects remain until deleted or lifecycle/retention settings are configured.

developer.notes: OCI Object Storage supports Console, CLI, REST API, S3 Compatibility API, Swift API, SDKs, and pre-authenticated request URLs.

name: Oshi.at
summary: Open-source anonymous ephemeral file-sharing service with command-line PUT uploads, per-upload management, optional instant destruction, and configurable/user-defined expiry.

free_model.notes: The upstream project describes OshiUpload as anonymous public file sharing FLOSS; no paid tier is described in the cited project source.

max_file_size.notes: The open-source engine uses a configurable upload-size setting; no current numeric public oshi.at cap is published in the reviewed project pages.

retention.notes: The config example sets default expiry to 1440 minutes and maximum expiry to 129600 minutes, while the README documents user-defined expiry and optional instant destruction after download.

storage.notes: OshiUpload is an ephemeral upload-and-share service, not a reusable personal storage account.

bandwidth.notes: The cited project pages do not publish a numeric public bandwidth quota.

account.benefits: Anonymous uploads are supported, with a management interface for each upload.

developer.notes: The upstream README advertises full command-line interaction, PUT uploads with curl -T, TCP uploads via netcat/telnet, and a management interface.

allowed_file_types.notes: The project is a general anonymous file-sharing engine; the cited README does not publish a narrow extension allowlist.

public_sharing.notes: The project describes anonymous public file sharing and says the synced source code is currently running at oshi.at.

security.notes: The upstream README emphasizes privacy and no collected logs, but it does not claim end-to-end encryption for uploaded files.

sources.label: OshiUpload GitHub repository

max_file_size_guest.notes: Anonymous browser uploads work without an account and are capped at 200 MB. The uploader source switches from plain HTTP to WebSockets above 50 MB.

storage_guest.notes: Storage is conditional. Anonymous uploads are supported and can optionally be organized through album-style management flows, but the official pages do not publish a reusable guest-storage quota.

account.benefits: No account is required. Uploads return delete links, and the public API also supports album creation with an ID and secret for later editing.

developer.notes: The public API docs include curl examples for file upload, deletion, metadata lookup, album creation, health checks, and removal requests.

security.notes: The features page says files are stored encrypted in Amazon S3, never written to disk unencrypted, and are served over HTTPS. The service does not claim end-to-end encryption.

name: Stashr
summary: Guest-friendly file-sharing host with 500 MB files, selectable expiry or download limits, and optional client-side encryption.

free_model.notes: This candidate has previously been verified for a free, guest, or free-account path. Recheck first-party sources before publishing or editing the row.

max_file_size.notes: The homepage and FAQ state a 500 MB maximum file size.

retention.notes: Retention is conditional. Uploads can be set to 1 hour, 6 hours, 12 hours, 1 day, 7 days, 30 days, or forever. The FAQ says files are stored indefinitely by default, while the terms describe 30 days if no expiration time is set.

storage.notes: Storage is conditional. Stashr stores files permanently by default unless the uploader chooses an expiration or download limit, but anonymous uploads are not tied to a reusable personal storage quota and the public pages do not publish one.

bandwidth.notes: Unlimited. The FAQ says all users get maximum upload and download speeds, with no throttling and no premium tiers. The service still applies per-IP upload rate limits and abuse-prevention controls.

account.benefits: No account is required for basic uploads. Free accounts add a dashboard, file management, and download statistics.

developer.notes: The cited public pages do not document a public API or CLI workflow.

security.notes: Stashr offers optional client-side AES-256-GCM encryption before upload. Password protection is separate from encryption, and the public pages do not claim that all files are end-to-end encrypted by default.

name: Wasabi
summary: Rejected object-storage lead: Wasabi has excellent S3-compatible APIs and large-object support, but it is paid/trial object storage rather than ongoing free-first file hosting.

free_model.notes: Wasabi pricing is paid object storage; official docs mention a free trial capped at 1 TB, but no ongoing free storage tier.

max_file_size.notes: Wasabi's official guide says individual objects can be up to five terabytes; single HTTP PUT uploads are capped at five gigabytes.

retention.notes: No automatic file expiry applies to normal stored objects by default. Wasabi has a 90-day minimum storage duration for billing, and bucket lifecycle/object-lock settings can alter deletion behavior.

storage.notes: The official object guide says total stored volume is unlimited, but the free trial is capped at 1 TB and Pay-as-You-Go billing has a 1 TB monthly minimum charge.

bandwidth.notes: Wasabi lists ingress, egress, and API requests as free under policy limits; free egress is considered reasonable when monthly egress is less than or equal to active storage volume.

account.benefits: An account is required to create buckets, access keys, and use S3-compatible object storage.

developer.notes: Wasabi documents S3-compatible APIs, AWS Signature Version 4 authentication, SDK compatibility, and account/statistics APIs.

allowed_file_types.notes: Wasabi stores generic objects/files in buckets. The cited guide says objects can be text, photo, video, etc.; it warns about certain filename characters rather than publishing an extension blocklist.

public_sharing.notes: Wasabi supports object actions such as sharing with a presigned URL and making files public or private.

security.notes: The API reference uses HTTPS service endpoints, but the cited pages do not claim general end-to-end encryption.

sources.label: Wikipedia comparison of file hosting services
sources.notes: Third-party seed list only. The article comparison table lists this provider, but every field still needs first-party verification before publication.

sources.notes: Official FAQ lists $6.99/TB/month pay-as-you-go storage, free ingress/egress/API requests subject to policies, 1 TB minimum monthly charge, and 90-day minimum storage duration.

sources.label: Wasabi API reference center
sources.notes: Official API center documents S3, WACM Connect, Stats, and Wasabi AiR APIs and S3-compatible SDK usage.

sources.label: Wasabi buckets and objects guide
sources.notes: Official product guide documents 5 TB maximum objects, unlimited total volume, 1 TB free-trial cap, public/private object actions, and presigned URLs.

name: Zmanda Cloud Backup
summary: Rejected enterprise-backup lead: Zmanda Cloud Backup is paid/trial backup and managed cloud storage, not a general ongoing free file host.

free_model.notes: Zmanda pricing lists paid Business, Business Plus, and Enterprise plans with Try for free CTAs; Zmanda Cloud Storage promotes a 14-day Zmanda Pro trial.

max_file_size.notes: No public single-file upload cap was found. Zmanda is modeled around workloads, file shares, databases, Microsoft 365, and managed backup storage.

retention.notes: No numeric public file-retention term was found for hosted files. The product focuses on backup retention/archiving rather than public file-host link expiry.

storage.notes: Zmanda Cloud Storage is paid per TB rather than a fixed free quota; official pricing lists Zmanda Cloud Storage at $6.49/TB/month on Business and $6.99/TB/month on Business Plus.

bandwidth.notes: No public bandwidth quota was found in the cited pricing or cloud-storage pages.

account.benefits: A business/trial account or sales flow is required to use Zmanda Pro, backup workloads, and Zmanda Cloud Storage.

developer.notes: The pricing page lists paid webhooks, custom automation scripts, and SIEM integration, but no public free-first upload API or CLI host workflow was verified.

allowed_file_types.notes: Zmanda protects workloads, file shares, and databases; the cited pages do not publish a public upload-hosting extension allowlist or blocklist.

public_sharing.notes: The cited pages describe backup, recovery, managed storage, and disaster recovery workflows, not public file-sharing links.

security.notes: The cited public pages do not claim general end-to-end encryption for hosted files.

sources.label: Wikipedia comparison of online backup services
sources.notes: Third-party seed list only. The article comparison table lists this provider, but every field still needs first-party verification before publication.

sources.label: Zmanda pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page lists paid per-device/per-TB plans and trial CTAs.

sources.label: Zmanda Cloud Storage
sources.notes: Official product page positions Zmanda Cloud Storage as enterprise managed cloud storage with a free 14-day Zmanda Pro trial CTA.

reason: Rejected because Zmanda Cloud Backup is enterprise backup with paid plans and trials, not a general ongoing free file-hosting service.

name: Zofile
summary: Account-managed file host with login-gated downloads, encrypted-storage claims, verified uploads, and XFileSharing-style sharing plus expiry behavior.

free_model.notes: This rejected candidate is not classified for free-model display; see the rejection reason for the review outcome.

max_file_size.notes: The accessible official pages do not publish a universal upload-size cap; the current service is account-managed and presents per-file download pages rather than a clear public max.

retention.notes: Retention remains conditional. The previously cited FAQ URL now returns 404, and the currently reachable official pages do not publish a clean expiry timer.

storage.notes: The current homepage presents Zofile as online storage and remote backup capacity, but it does not publish a neat account-storage quota.

bandwidth.notes: The official homepage account header says traffic available today is unlimited, while download pages are account-gated and resume-supported, so transfer behavior is clearly account-managed rather than a pure blank unknown.

account.benefits: The current official surface is account-managed: the homepage emphasizes login, sign up, upload, my files, and premium, while sample download pages say users must log in to download.

developer.notes: The accessible official pages describe end-user file-hosting behavior only and do not publish a public developer API or CLI workflow.

security.notes: The official pages are served over HTTPS, and sample file pages explicitly label stored files as encrypted storage, but there is no end-to-end encryption claim.
